key_assumptions.input: Starting free cash flow per share
key_assumptions.source: FY2025 operating cash flow $1.39B less capital spending $0.58B = $0.81B, divided by 462.3M shares outstanding (Q3 FY2026 10-Q)
Berry synergies phase in; 9M FY2026 revenue $17.1B (+72% YoY); operating income $1.25B (+36% YoY)

open_questions: Filed owner cash **$1.75/sh** (FY2025 OCF $1.39B − capex $0.58B ÷ 462M shares); synergy uplift not in Lawrence base until evidenced in MD&A.
Synergy dollar target cited in merger materials should be verified against MD&A when extracted from full proxy/earnings deck.
Post-merger equity offerings (424B5): assess dilution impact on per-share owner cash.
thesis: Amcor plc (NYSE: **AMCR**) is one of the world's largest packaging manufacturers. **[Fact]** It develops and produces flexible packaging (films, pouches, lidding), rigid packaging (bottles, containers, closures), and specialty cartons for food, beverage, healthcare, home care, and personal-care customers. **[Fact]** The company completed an all-stock merger with **Berry Global Group** on **April 30, 2025**, exchanging **7.25** Amcor ordinary shares for each Berry share and expanding the listed equity base to about **462 million** shares outstanding at March 31, 2026. **[Fact]** FY2025 net sales were **$15.0 billion** (up **10%** versus the prior year on a reported basis) and pro forma combined revenue would have been about **$23.2 billion** per the acquisition footnote in the FY2025 annual report. **[Fact]** The business reports two operating segments: **Flexibles** and **Rigid Packaging**, with nine-month FY2026 segment net sales of **$8.0 billion** and **$2.4 billion**, respectively. **[Inference]** Owner economics are real but noisy in the integration year: FY2025 operating cash flow was **$1.39 billion** while capital spending was **$0.58 billion**, implying about **$1.75 per share** of owner cash on the current share count, before merger-related restructuring and elevated integration capex in recent quarters.
why_market_wrong: There is **no classic dated mispricing signal** (HK predictive attribute: **none**). **[Inference]** Generalists may still price Amcor as a low-growth commodity packager with merger indigestion, underweighting the step-change in scale, customer diversification, and synergy potential from Berry. **[Fact]** Nine-month FY2026 net sales rose **72%** to **$17.1 billion** and operating income rose **36%** to **$1.25 billion** versus the prior-year period, showing the combined revenue base is already flowing through the income statement. **[Inference]** Bears may overweight near-term earnings dilution (FY2025 diluted continuing EPS **$0.32** versus **$0.51** prior year) and **$13.8 billion** of long-term debt without giving credit to resin pass-through mechanics and multi-year cost synergy programs typical in packaging roll-ups. Return here is **normalized owner cash at price through integration**, not a sum-of-the-parts on goodwill.
